key_insight: Constrain the LLM to parsing, geometry handling, and validation while keeping the finite-element solver fully deterministic and human-written.
engineering_relevance: This architecture makes natural-language setup of multi-physics FEniCS simulations more practical without placing the numerical core of the analysis under LLM control.
method: LLM converts prompts into structured JSON and only generates Gmsh code for non-catalog geometries.
A deterministic dispatcher selects one of five prebuilt FEniCS/UFL templates: linear elasticity, hyperelasticity, elastoplasticity, thermo-mechanical coupling, or phase-field fracture.
Retry feedback is used only for parsing and geometry generation; weak forms and solver code remain fixed.
evidence_signal: The parser benchmark reached 100% final valid parses after retries, and the deterministic templates matched analytical and published benchmarks with sub-percent errors in smooth cases and about 2–5% in harder nonlinear cases.
watch_item: The approach still depends on reliable prompt parsing and geometry generation, and custom-geometry failures can remain unrecovered in edge cases.
paper_title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
